title: Cyclobalanopsis kerrii
intro: Cyclobalanopsis kerrii, a species of deciduous tree native to Southeast Asia, belongs to the beech family (Fagaceae) and is also known as the Kerr's chestnut oak. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyclobalanopsis kerrii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Cyclobalanopsis kerrii is classified within the family Fagaceae. This species belongs to the genus Cyclobalanopsis and the order Fagales. The Fagaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the beech family. Cyclobalanopsis kerrii is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in China and Myanmar. It is a type of evergreen tree that thrives in mountainous regions with cool and humid climates.
